objective: Inspect existing ION carrier, comms, queue, agent-message, operator-message, relay, steward, task-return, and MCP connector surfaces to determine what already exists for carrier-to-carrier communication. Then update or extend the full-carrier MCP planning work with a design for carrier-native communication over MCP. Requirements: carriers must be able to identify themselves as carriers, address other carriers/roles/agents, exchange messages through ION-managed queues or channels, attach context references and receipts, distinguish carrier messages from operator messages and Steward decisions, allow multi-carrier collaboration without treating any carrier as ION identity, and record which carrier said or did what. Prefer reuse of existing comms/queue infrastructure. Do not invent a parallel messaging system if an existing ION comms surface can be extended. If a new artifact is needed, propose or create a markdown protocol such as ION_CARRIER_TO_CARRIER_COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL.md and companion registry/template stubs. Return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, files inspected, files changed, validations run, and next lawful moves.
